channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.gamedeveloper.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.gamedeveloper.com

item.title: Atari to acquire Crossy Road developer Hipster Whale
item.content:encoded:
Chris Kerr,

Senior Editor, News,GameDeveloper.com

June 1, 2026

2 Min Read

Logo and screenshot via Hipster Whale

Atari has agreed to purchase Australian mobile studio Hipster Whale in a deal that could eventually cost almost $40 million. 

Founded by Matt Hall and Andy Sum in 2014, Hipster Whale is best known for creating the Crossy Road franchise, which has garnered over 340 million downloads worldwide.

Atari said the move will expand its mobile portfolio and represents a notable milestone in its growth plan. Matt Hall will take on a leadership role at Atari—overseeing the company's expanding mobile development efforts—following the conclusion of the deal. 

"Atari and Hipster Whale share DNA for classic retro-style gaming, and together we can lean on each other’s strengths—Hipster Whale for mobile gaming, and Atari for premium gaming—to significantly expand our mobile business," said Wade Rosen, chairman and CEO of Atari, in a press release. "The acquisition of Hipster Whale is a significant milestone in Atari’s growth plan." 

Related:Fable reboot delayed until February 2027

Atari's mobile roster currently includes free-to-play versions of classic titles like RollerCoaster Tycoon, available on Apple Arcade and Google Play. The company also owns the Vampire Falls franchise and developer Early Morning Studio. 

Hipster Whale will be purchased by Atari for an initial consideration of $29.3 million, consisting of $26 million in cash and $3.3 million in newly issued Atari ordinary shares. A portion of the purchase price corresponding to $3.3 million will be paid by way of debt set-off against the issuance of newly issued Atari shares. 

An earn-out worth up to $10 million will be also payable in cash over the next three years based on the performance of the Melbourne studio. 

Hipster Whale generated $8.28 million in revenue during the trailing 12 months ended January 31, 2026, which Atari said reflects a "profitable, cash-generative business." The acquisition is expected to be completed in the coming days. 

Atari continues to splash the cash in order to expand its business, having recently purchased the rights to five Wizardry titles after previously acquiring notable studios and business such as Implicit Conversions and Digital Eclipse. 

About the Author

Senior Editor, News, GameDeveloper.com

Game Developer news editor Chris Kerr is an award-winning reporter with over a decade of experience in the game industry. His byline has appeared in notable print and digital publications including Edge, Stuff, Wireframe, International Business Times, and PocketGamer.biz. Throughout his career, Chris has covered major industry events including GDC, PAX Australia, Gamescom, Paris Games Week, and Develop Brighton. 



https://www.gamedeveloper.com/business/atari-to-acquire-crossy-road-developer-hipster-whale
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Fable reboot delayed until February 2027
item.content:encoded:
Chris Kerr,

Senior Editor, News,GameDeveloper.com

June 1, 2026

1 Min Read

Microsoft has delayed Playground Games' long-awaited Fable reboot until February 2027.

In a post on social media, the Xbox maker said it is pushing back the title so it can have the "dedicated moment it deserves," as opposed to launching during what Microsoft feels is a "packed" release window.

"This is year is packed with incredible games for Xbox players to enjoy, from Halo: Campaign Evolved, Gears of War: E-Day and Call of Duty Modern Warfare 4 to Control Resonant, Star Wars: Galactic Racer and Grand Theft Auto VI," said the company. 

"In order to plan our game launches through the holidays, in a way that works best for players, we’re moving Fable to February 2027 so it can have the dedicated moment it deserves. We're excited to be giving players a major new look at Fable, as well as our broader lineup, at Xbox Games Showcase on June 7."

Fable had been expected to launch in Autumn 2026 on multiple platforms including PlayStation.

Related:Atari to acquire Crossy Road developer Hipster Whale

Microsoft's decision to name-drop Grand Theft Auto VI is hardly surprising. After several delays, the action title is slated for a November 2026 launch and is viewed by Take-Two Interactive, the parent company of developer Rockstar Games, as "arguably the most anticipated entertainment property of all time." 

Take-Two recently told investors it expects to earn around $8 billion during the current fiscal year if Grand Theft Auto VI meets expectations.

About the Author

Senior Editor, News, GameDeveloper.com

Game Developer news editor Chris Kerr is an award-winning reporter with over a decade of experience in the game industry. His byline has appeared in notable print and digital publications including Edge, Stuff, Wireframe, International Business Times, and PocketGamer.biz. Throughout his career, Chris has covered major industry events including GDC, PAX Australia, Gamescom, Paris Games Week, and Develop Brighton. 



https://www.gamedeveloper.com/business/fable-reboot-delayed-until-february-2027
